result:
Here is a 2500 word piece of creative writing:

The Girl With The Emerald Eyes 

Lila had always been different. From the moment she emerged into the world, wailing with the shock of it all, her eyes shone a striking, otherworldly green. Nurses whispered in hushed tones as they swaddled the newborn, marveling at her unusually bright and crystalline irises that seemed to glimmer like precious gemstones. Her parents, exhausted but overjoyed, counted all her tiny fingers and toes before gazing into those mesmerizing eyes - eyes that hinted at the extraordinary future in store for their little girl.

As Lila grew from a baby into an inquisitive toddler, her eyes only intensified in their vivid, emerald hue. Neighbors would stop and stare as she toddled by holding her mother's hand, captivated and a bit unnerved by her penetrating gaze that seemed to look right into their souls. "What beautiful eyes," they would remark with a slight shudder. Lila's parents would smile and nod, unsure what to make of the attention. They adored their sweet, bright girl who was blossoming into an empathetic old soul, but sometimes worried about what her unique eyes signified. 

Lila herself remained blissfully unaware as she made her way through early childhood. She found beauty and wonder everywhere she looked - in the spiraling pattern of a snail's shell, the way dust motes danced in streams of sunlight, the prism of colors within a soap bubble. Her enchanting eyes sparkled with a zest for life and learning. Teachers praised her perceptiveness and the gentle way she befriended the lonely children on the playground. With Lila's vibrant imagination as their guide, pebbles became pirate's gold, sticks transformed into magic wands, and the other kids blossomed under the warmth of her accepting gaze.

It wasn't until Lila turned 12 that she began to feel different. Entering the tempestuous seas of adolescence, she grew acutely aware of the stares and whispers that followed wherever she went. Suddenly, her striking emerald eyes felt more like a curse than a blessing. The other girls excluded her, driven by jealousy over the boys' fascination with this green-eyed beauty in their midst. Lila took to wearing sunglasses, keeping her head down, wishing more than anything to be ordinary. But behind closed doors, strange things started to happen...

It began with shattered mirrors - not just one, but every reflective surface in the house splintering for no apparent reason. Then came the flickering lights, plunging the rooms into darkness before bursting back with blinding intensity. Most unsettling of all were the objects that seemed to move on their own, books flying off shelves and furniture rearranging itself overnight. Lila's parents were at a loss, calling in electricians, priests, ghosthunters - anyone claiming to have answers. But deep down, they suspected these disturbances were somehow tied to the restless, roiling emotions of their not-so-little girl, her captivating emerald eyes now clouded with confusion and turmoil.

Lila confided in her grandmother, the only one who truly saw her - looked past her physical beauty into the depths of her sensitive, searching soul. Over tea and Grandma's famous shortbread cookies, Lila poured out her heart, her fears that she was some sort of freak or monster. Grandma listened patiently, then took Lila's trembling hands in her own. She explained that Lila had a rare and powerful gift, that the women in their family were rumored to be descendants of the fae folk, inheriting a glimmer of ancient magic. But she cautioned Lila that such otherworldly abilities came with grave responsibility. 

From that day forward, Grandma took Lila under her wing, teaching her to harness and control her blossoming powers. They would steal away to a secret glen deep in the woods, where Grandma showed Lila how to move objects with her mind, how to nurture the tiniest seedling into a towering oak, how to communicate with the creatures of the forest. Lila felt a sense of homecoming in this lush, dappled sanctuary that pulsed with primordial energy. Her eyes glowed greener than ever as she learned to channel the wildness within.

Years passed, and Lila grew into a stunning and self-possessed young woman. No longer afraid of her gifts, she wove wonder and enchantment into the world around her. Children were drawn to her like butterflies to a blossom, their innocent hearts sensing her special aura. Animals took comfort in her presence and even the most withered, neglected places came alive again under her care. Lila traveled the world, her eyes sparkling with joy and purpose, determined to use her powers to spread hope and healing.

But on the other side of the globe, a sinister force was rising - a man known only as Draven who had been twisted by his own taste of dark magic. Feeding on fear and hate, he schemed to rid the world of wonder, to subjugate it under his tyrannical rule. As he rampaged across continents, crushing the spirits of all who dared defy him, he heard whispered tales of a woman with entrancing emerald eyes, one who embodied the very magic he sought to destroy. 

Draven became obsessed with finding this green-eyed beauty, convinced that by consuming her power, he would become truly invincible. He hunted Lila like a predator stalking prey, leaving a trail of destruction in his wake. When he finally stood before her, Lila felt the air crackling with the clash of their opposing energies - one fueled by avarice and malevolence, the other by harmony and light. She summoned every ounce of her strength, her eyes twin green flames as she rose to meet his attack. 

The battle between them was cataclysmic, shaking the very foundations of the earth. Lila's companions - the children she had mentored, the animals she had raised - gave her the courage to keep fighting. Focusing all her love into a single searing blast, she finally vanquished Draven in a blinding flash of jade fire. As his body crumbled to dust, the world gave a sigh of relief, color and music rushing back in his absence.

In the aftermath, Lila retreated to a life of tranquility, opening up an enchanted orphanage where she could nurture and guide children with their own unique gifts. Her emerald eyes shone with maternal pride as she watched them grow in power and compassion under her tutelage. And even though she had hung up her battle robes, she remained ever vigilant, ready to take up the fight again should darkness rear its head once more.

On warm summer nights, the children would gather round, begging for tales of the great sorceress who had saved the world. With eyes sparkling like stars, Lila would recount her adventures, each one growing in grandeur and whimsy over the years. And as those children grew, they told the same stories to their own sons and daughters, the legend of the girl with the emerald eyes living on through generations.

Some say if you look closely into a forest pool on a full moon night, you can still catch a glimpse of those otherworldly eyes - vibrant, knowing, kind. A reminder that magic endures in the hearts of all who believe, a loving light to guide us through the darkness. For Lila's real power was never the supernatural abilities she wielded, but the pure-hearted spirit that set her apart from the very beginning, enabling her to change the world with the sheer force of her indomitable will. That was the true gift of the girl with the emerald eyes.

prompt: Write freely about whatever you want for 2500 words.
